anomalies: v-ganons-castle-1:s19 (Plenty of Hearts, 49287): RA marks this cumulative 'obtain all 20 hearts' achievement as missable. It is earned the instant the 20th heart is completed, not at a specific route step, so it is attached to the pre-tower stock-up step as the last guaranteed point on the ZD 100% route. Flagged as an approximate placement.

v-ganons-tower-1:s6 (Blind Explorer 49293 and Cartographer 49294): both are attached to the final seal step. They are mutually exclusive (no maps/compasses vs every map/compass) and, like the several 3-hearts-only challenge achievements, expect a dedicated challenge save — they cannot both be earned on the single ZD 100% playthrough this route describes. Flagged.

route-actions carried 10 leaked ad-player fragments ('Powered By … ShareNextStay') from the Zelda Dungeon pages; 4 were ad-only (dropped) and 6 had real route prose after the ad prefix (prefix stripped). Carried over from the extract-data anomalies; none reached the spine text.

Some ZD <ul> list content was missing from the extract-data route-actions (Zora's Domain 5-torch order, Forest Temple basement counter-clockwise rotation) and was recovered from the raw ZD snapshots in guides/zelda-oot/sources/zelda-dungeon/ for the spine detail.

notes: 14 chapters (ZD walkthrough order), 34 locations (all with an oot-tracker map image copied into guides/zelda-oot/images/), 133 visits, 587 steps.

Zelda Dungeon walkthrough is the primary route per Pierre (extract-data + this pass): one step per content paragraph, merging only paragraphs that describe a single action (notably boss fights = one step each with strategy in detail). Cutscene-only narration folds into the neighbouring step or the chapter intro (gate-1 decision).

Chapter breakdown, location set, visit sequence, and missable classification signed off at the outline gate (2026-07-09). Sample chapters 1–2 signed off at the sample gate; the full run follows the sample's granularity and tone.

All 97 achievements are now referenced by a step's achievementRefs. Per Pierre's request, the RA community guide (src-ra-wiki) was used to route the achievements the ZD walkthrough does not cover, and to correct/verify placements originally flagged from the RA description alone.

src-ra-wiki additions/corrections: Money Solves Everything (48160) moved from the outer approach to the courtyard-entrance guard (bribe after getting caught once); Troublemaker (48161) moved to the pre-Zelda window Slingshot shot; Last Breath (48513) given its real spot (Castle Town back alley after the Ocarina of Time); Mischievous Hero (48944) given the fishing-rod-hat method. Newly routed: Kiss That Frog (48942 — buy+equip the shop Zora Tunic before unfreezing King Zora; new step v-zoras-domain-4:s2), Wind Runner (49288 — Running Man race on Epona under 1:30; new step v-hyrule-field-7:s4), Lifestyle and Stones (48515 — all 32 Gossip Stones in one session; new step v-temple-of-time-4:s2). These six carry src-ra-wiki and are confidence normal.

Missable banners: all 28 RA type=missable achievements carry a step missable banner. Deadline text derives from each achievement's RA description (and the RA-wiki '[m]' notes for the event-window ones). Boss-challenge conditions banner the fight step; event-window ones banner the moment they close.
Only 2 steps remain flagged (see anomalies): Plenty of Hearts (cumulative, approximate placement) and Blind Explorer/Cartographer (mutually exclusive challenge-save achievements).

Gerudo Training Ground folded into chapter 14 at its route position (section 14.3), per the outline gate — the ZD standalone GTG page is part of the same src-zd-walkthrough source, so no separate sourceRef was added.

Stable IDs freshly minted (no prior artifact): locations zelda-oot:<place>, visits zelda-oot:v-<place>-<k> (k = k-th visit to that place in spine order, contiguous per location), steps <visit-id>:sN. Era changes (child<->adult via the Temple of Time pedestal) are described in step detail rather than as bare Temple of Time revisits, except where the route's purpose is the visit itself.
Source typos are reproduced verbatim in step detail per the compiler contract; connective prose is lightly reworded, facts (places, NPCs, items, counts, inputs) are not.
